     The Numbers Business Report

Eurotrip (2004)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,718,223Details
International Box Office $3,000,000
Worldwide Box Office $20,718,223
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After a brutal breakup at a high-school graduation party, lovelorn Ohio teen Scott Thomas goes on a quest across Europe to find his sexy German pen pal, Mieke. Joining him are his brainless buddy and a pair of twins they meet up with in Paris. Scott's quest does not turn out to be easy, however, and the backpackers become embroiled in many farcical situations as they hop from country to country.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,711,384 (37.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.64 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:85.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,512 opening theaters/2,544 max. theaters, 3.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $34,123,985

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 20th, 2004 (Wide) by Dreamworks SKG
International Releases: August 12th, 2004 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: June 9th, 2004 by Warner Bros / Paramount
MPA Rating: R
Running Time: 92 minutes
Plot point: Coming of Age, Relationships Gone Wrong, Road Trip, Romance, Twins
Social setting: Graduation
Subgenre: Teenage Sex Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: DreamWorks Pictures, Montecito Picture Company
Production Countries: United States, Czech Republic
Languages: English, French, German, Italian, Japanese

Synopsis

Joining him are his brainless buddy and a pair of twins they meet up with in Paris. Scott's quest does not turn out to be easy, however, and the backpackers become embroiled in many farcical situations as they hop from country to country.
